raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California is famous for its cannabis culture driven by the state’s early adoption of medical cannabis and expertise in cultivation.",
    "Amsterdam's cannabis cafes thrive as a part of the city’s unique social structure.",
    "The social nature of cannabis can be capitalized on through coffee, food, and live music.",
    "Current California law makes it illegal for cannabis retailers to sell non-cannabis-infused foods.",
    "AB 1775 allows cannabis retailers to diversify their business away from the limited dispensary model.",
    "There is no good reason from an economic, health, or safety standpoint to prevent authorized cannabis retail stores from selling coffee and sandwiches.",
    "The previous version of the bill, AB 374, was vetoed by Governor Newsom due to insufficient protections for cannabis workers.",
    "AB 1775 closes a loophole by allowing cannabis lounges to serve fresh food instead of just pre-packaged food or food received from delivery services.",
    "Safety provisions were recently added to AB 1775 to better protect both workers and patrons in cannabis lounges."
  ]
}
```
